title: SAT Practice - Performance Feedback Messages
description: Funny, slightly mocking motivational messages based on performance

messages.text: Are you allergic to correct answers? Because you're avoiding them like they're contagious.
I've seen Magic 8-Balls with better accuracy. At least they get it right sometimes.
Plot twist: The questions were in English. Did anyone tell you?
Your score is so low, it's practically underground. Should we send a search party?
Even a broken clock is right twice a day. You might want to aim for that.
Fun fact: Random guessing should get you 25% correct. You're... impressively below that.
The good news: You can only go up from here! The bad news: Everything else.
You know what? A coin flip would literally do better. At least coins are consistent.
Your answers are giving 'eenie meenie miney moe' energy.
I'm starting to think you're doing this wrong on purpose. It's actually impressive.
At this rate, you'll get into college... as a cautionary tale in their admissions training.
Good news: You beat random guessing! Bad news: Not by much.
Your strategy seems to be 'choose the answer that FEELS right.' How's that working out?
Fun idea: Try picking the OPPOSITE of what you think. Can't hurt at this point.
Would you like me to pull up some vocational school applications? Just kidding... kind of.
You're in the danger zone - not quite failing, but definitely not passing the vibe check.
The SAT Gods are sending you a message. That message is: 'Try harder.'
You're so close to average that average is ghosting you.
Community college is looking pretty good right about now, huh?
At this rate, your college essay is going to be: 'Dear Admissions, Let me explain...'
You know what rhymes with your score? Door. As in, the exit door.
Your performance is giving 'I'll just retake it in the fall' energy.
Congrats! You've achieved... adequacy. Your parents are thrilled. (They're not.)
You're the participation trophy of SAT scores.
70%? That's a C. But hey, C's get degrees! ...At *some* colleges.
You're not great, but you're not terrible. You're like the middle seat on an airplane.
State school? State school.
You're like a pizza that's okay but not great. Nobody's excited, but it'll do.
Your score says 'I studied... the night before.'
Solid 7/10. Perfect score for someone who wants a solid 7/10 life.
So close! And yet, so far. Story of your life?
You're like the person who loses by ONE point on a game show. Painful.
B+ energy. Good, but your Asian parents are still disappointed.
You can see the finish line! Too bad you stopped for a snack break.
At this rate, you'll get into your safety school! Isn't that... nice?
You're good, but are you '800 on the SAT' good? Survey says: Not yet.
Your score is the equivalent of 'we need to talk.' It's fine, but IS it?
Alumni interviewer: 'So, what makes you special?' You: 'Well...'
You're THIS close to greatness. Unfortunately, 'this close' doesn't count.
Wow! You're in the 90s! Too bad you're aiming for the high 90s.
You're like the straight-A student who got one A-. Devastating.
Great score! ...If you were applying to less competitive schools.
You're knocking on excellence's door. Unfortunately, excellence isn't home.
Ivy League schools: 'It's not you, it's... actually, yeah, it's you.'
Your score is good. Great, even! But you want PERFECT, remember?
So close to perfection that perfection is starting to feel awkward.
You're the understudy to greatness. One day, maybe you'll be the star.
Stanford: 'We'll... keep your application on file.' (They won't.)
ONE POINT AWAY. That's gotta sting.
You're like the Olympic athlete with the silver medal. Great! But also... ouch.
So you're telling me you studied THIS HARD and STILL missed some?
You know what's worse than failing? Being ONE question away from perfect.
The SAT Gods looked at your test and said 'Lol no, not today.'
At this point, the wrong answers are personal attacks.
You were SO CLOSE to flexing a perfect score. Instead, you're just... really good.
That one question you missed? It's going to haunt you forever.
MIT: 'Impressive! ...But did you notice our acceptance rate?'
800! You did it! Now do it again to prove it wasn't a fluke.
Perfect score! Your parents are already updating their Facebook status.
Congratulations! You may now rest... after you take 47 AP classes.
FLAWLESS. Now go touch grass before you forget what sunlight looks like.
You've peaked. It's all downhill from here. Enjoy this moment.
Perfect score! Time to develop a personality!
800/800. You're the reason the curve is so harsh for everyone else. Thanks.
Amazing! Now the REAL pressure begins: Living up to this. Good luck!
You've achieved academic greatness. Your reward? More studying!
Perfection unlocked! Achievement: 'Insufferable At Parties'

all_wrong.title: Statistically Impossible 🚨
messages: Getting EVERYTHING wrong is actually harder than getting everything right. Congrats?
This is... this is actually impressive. In the worst way possible.
I don't even know what to say. I'm just... I'm impressed by the commitment to failure.
Are you a genius playing 4D chess by deliberately getting everything wrong?
Your score is so bad it broke the motivational message system.

improved.title: Growth Mindset! 📈
messages: You improved! See, you CAN learn! Who knew?
Look at you, getting better! Still not great, but better!
Progress! Slow, painful progress, but progress nonetheless.
You're climbing the ladder of success! ...Very slowly.
Improvement detected! There's hope for you yet!

declined.title: Going Backwards? 📉
messages: Wait, you got WORSE? That takes skill.
Your score is going down like my GPA sophomore year.
Are you okay? Blink twice if you need help.
This is the wrong direction. The goal is UP.
Maybe take a nap and try again?

struggling: Math is hard. You know what's harder? Explaining your score to your parents.
Having trouble with math? There's always... *checks notes* ...liberal arts!
Math: Where knowing the answer and getting the answer are two different things.
doing_well: Great math score! You're officially better at numbers than understanding people.
Math genius! Too bad that won't help you make friends.

struggling: Struggling with English? Maybe try... pictures?
Grammar is hard when you don't read anything except TikTok captions.
The English language called. It wants to know why you're treating it like this.
doing_well: Good at English! Someone has to write the apologetic LinkedIn posts for STEM majors.
Excellent verbal skills! Perfect for explaining why you didn't get into your dream school.

random_roasts: Your score is giving 'I'll study tomorrow' energy.
At least you're consistent... consistently mediocre.
Your test-taking strategy: Chaos. Results: Also chaos.
If confidence was points, you'd have an 800. Unfortunately...
You know what they say: C's get degrees! (They also get judged.)
Your score and your screen time are inversely proportional.
Fun fact: The SAT was designed to measure aptitude. Yours is... measured.
Your performance is a testament to... something. Not sure what yet.
Remember: It's not about the score, it's about... actually, it's totally about the score.
This score is a character-building experience! (That's what parents say when you fail.)

taking_too_long: At this pace, you'll finish the SAT when you're ready to retire.
Speed matters! Unless you're planning to take a gap decade.
You know there's a time limit, right? ...Right?
rushing: You finished fast! ...And answered incorrectly. Interesting strategy.
What's the hurry? Hot date with disappointment?

_notes.context_tags: Messages tagged with context: ['consumer_only'] must be filtered out when school_mode is active.
_notes.usage: Pass schoolMode boolean to getRoastForScore() to filter.
